file.content:
# Refactor API to use Bun.serve() instead of tsx

## Context

The API currently runs via `tsx watch src/index.ts`, which uses Node.js's `createServer` from `node:http` with `@orpc/server/node` RPCHandler. Since the monorepo already uses Bun as its package manager, we can simplify the stack by using Bun's native HTTP server (`Bun.serve()`) and the fetch-based oRPC adapter (`@orpc/server/fetch`), removing the `tsx` dependency entirely.

## Changes

### 1. Update `apps/api/src/index.ts`
- Replace `node:http` createServer with `Bun.serve()`
- Switch from `@orpc/server/node` RPCHandler to `@orpc/server/fetch` RPCHandler
- The fetch adapter's `handle()` returns `{ matched, response }` — return `response` when matched, otherwise return a 404 Response

### 2. Update `apps/api/package.json`
- Change `dev` script from `tsx watch src/index.ts` to `bun --watch src/index.ts`
- Remove `build` script (`tsc`) — Bun runs TS natively, no build step needed
- Remove `tsx` from devDependencies
- Remove `@types/node` from devDependencies (using Bun types instead)

### 3. Update `apps/api/tsconfig.json`
- Add `"types": ["bun-types"]` to pick up Bun globals (`Bun.serve`, etc.)
- Remove `"types": ["node"]`

### 4. Install `bun-types`
- Add `bun-types` as a devDependency in `apps/api`

## Files to modify
- `apps/api/src/index.ts` — server entry point
- `apps/api/package.json` — scripts & dependencies
- `apps/api/tsconfig.json` — TypeScript types

## Verification
- Run `bun --watch apps/api/src/index.ts` and confirm the server starts on port 4010
- `curl http://localhost:4010/rpc/health` returns `{"status":"ok","timestamp":...}`
- Run `turbo dev` from root and confirm both API and web still work together

task.description: Start API server, test health endpoint and 404, then stop

content.text: The server starts and listens on port 4010. The `/nonexistent` path correctly returns 404. The `/rpc/health` route is being matched by oRPC (it returns a 405 "Method Not Supported" — that's because `curl` defaults to GET, and the oRPC health endpoint likely expects POST). Let me verify with a POST request.

content.text:
All changes are complete and verified. Here's a summary of what was done:

**`apps/api/src/index.ts`** — Replaced Node.js `createServer` with `Bun.serve()` and switched from `@orpc/server/node` to `@orpc/server/fetch` RPCHandler. The fetch adapter returns `{ matched, response }` directly instead of mutating the Node response object.

**`apps/api/package.json`** — Changed `dev` script to `bun --watch src/index.ts`, removed the `build` script (Bun runs TS natively), and swapped `tsx`/`@types/node` for `bun-types`.

**`apps/api/tsconfig.json`** — Switched types from `["node"]` to `["bun-types"]`.

**Verification results:**
- Server starts on port 4010
- `POST /rpc/health` returns `{"json":{"status":"ok","timestamp":...}}`
- Non-matching routes return 404
- `check-types` passes cleanly

how can i use this worktree locally? (aka run commands on this tree?)

content.text:
This workspace is just a regular directory at:

```
/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/gazed/oslo
```

You can open a terminal and `cd` into it to run any commands:

```bash
cd /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/gazed/oslo
bun install
bun run --filter @repo/api dev    # start API on :4010
bun run --filter web dev          # start frontend on :4011
turbo dev                         # start both
```

If it's a git worktree (linked to another repo), you can also open it directly in your editor:

```bash
code /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/gazed/oslo
```

Everything works the same as any normal project directory — the workspace is just a path on disk.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "Refactor API to use Bun.serve() instead of tsx" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Replace `node:http` createServer with `Bun.serve()` and switch from `@orpc/server/node` to `@orpc/server/fetch` adapter
- Remove `tsx` and `@types/node` dev dependencies in favor of `bun-types`
- Simplify dev script to `bun --watch src/index.ts` and remove unnecessary `build` script

## Test plan
- [x] Server starts on port 4010
- [x] `POST /rpc/health` returns `{"json":{"status":"ok","timestamp":...}}`
- [x] Non-matching routes return 404
- [x] `check-types` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
